enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a rambling vlog update from his Boulder apartment on February 27, 2015, showing off a motivational 'shrine' he built around his bike rollers with photos of pro cyclists organized by speed levels. He also talks about temporarily driving his roommate's dad's BMW, getting a new car, doing paperwork he's been avoiding, plans to do an hour power test on his rollers, and working on his cycling website while high.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: building a photo shrine of pro cyclists around his rollers helps him push harder
wants to standardize indoor vs outdoor speed comparisons to make progress transparent and measurable
makes it harder but manageable as long as you proofread afterward
it's about to break — been soldered so many times it's basically a solid block of metal, but he's not in a rush to replace it
open to using a fund-me campaign to get bike equipment in the name of science

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a fall start the other day while riding my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to know how fast I was when I was strong, so I created a conversion chart for my biking speed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I put up photos and created a shrine-like setup with lights to motivate myself while biking.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a chart with levels from international pro down to average commuter, calibrated for a 150-pound person on a triathlon bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I did a test last year and averaged 17.7 miles per hour, which translates to 235 miles per hour on a triathlon bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do an hour test tomorrow to see my current biking level.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once the snow melts, I’ll do a 40-kilometer loop and predict my time based on my current strength.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m motivated by the science of calibrating and standardizing my biking performance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might seek financial support through a funding platform to get a new bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My current bike is heavy and might break soon because it’s been soldered together many times.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ve been working on my website to make it more organized and explanatory.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I find it harder to write when I’m high, but I still prefer it because I can proofread later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My car broke down twice this winter, once when the wheel fell off and another time when the gas pedal fell off.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My roommate’s dad invented a new kind of plastic and started a company that became a Fortune 500 company.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My roommate doesn’t like driving, so he might let me use his BMW.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a new car with manual shifting and traction control, which excites me more than the motorcycle I had last summer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ve been putting off paperwork for a month, and my mom is getting upset about it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do my paperwork while on the rollers to multitask.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I forgot about an apple my mom gave me because I’m not used to eating apples.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ve been working late, sometimes until 2:30 AM, and only getting about five and a half hours of sleep.
